q: Electronics is the branch of physics and technology concerned with the design of circuits using transistors, integrated circuits, and microchips.
Active electrical components like resistors, capacitors, and inductors are capable of operating without an external power source.
Zener diode is a heavily doped semiconductor (silicon or germanium) pn junction diode.
A normal diode allows current to flow in both directions while a zener diode allows current to flow only in one direction.
Avalanche breakdown occurs at junctions which being lightly doped have wide depletion layers.
Zener breakdown occurs at junctions which being lightly doped have wide depletion layers.
The voltage drop across the zener diode always remains constant irrespective of the applied voltage.
A light emitting diode (LED) is known to be a semiconductor light source that emits light when it is reverse-biased.
The visible lights that an LED emits are usually orange, red, yellow, or green.
The commonly used invisible light for LED is ultraviolet light.
Gallium Arsenide (GaAs) is an exotic semiconductor compound used to produce infra-red LEDs with a wavelength of 850-940 nm.
Gallium Indium Nitride (GaInN) mixed with a phosphor coating is used to convert blue light to white light by a process called fluorescence.
LEDs directly produce white light through the use of exotic semiconductor compounds without the use of phosphor coatings.
LEDs have a fast action and no warmup time, with a response time of about $10 ns$.
The LED device is known to have a much narrower bandwidth (wavelength spread) compared to the laser.
A photodiode is a semiconductor device that converts light into electrical current when photons are absorbed.
Photodiodes work in forward biased mode to generate photocurrent when illuminated with light.
The magnitude of the photocurrent depends on the intensity of incident light.
A small amount of current called dark current is produced due to the flow of minority carriers when no light is falling on the photodiode.
A light dependent resistor (LDR) is also called light sensitive variable resistors.
In the dark or in dim light, the material of the LDR disc has a relatively large number of free electrons in it.
The dark resistance of an LDR is quite high, reaching approximately $1 M\Omega$.
In the bright light, the resistance of an LDR is low and remains constant at several hundred $k\Omega$.
Special semiconductor crystals such as cadmium sulfide or lead sulfide are used to make photoresistors.
A thermistor is a temperature sensor that exhibits a small change in resistance proportional to a change in temperature.
In the Negative Temperature Coefficient (NTC) thermistor, the resistance decreases as the temperature increases.
Positive Temperature Coefficient (PTC) thermistors are used to break the circuit as the temperature decreases below a set point.
Liquid crystals in a liquid crystal display (LCD) emit light directly while using a backlight or reflector.
A polarizer is an optical device that can convert an unpolarized light wave into a polarize light wave by blocking all other vibrations.
LCDs consume much more power than LEDs and gas-plasma displays because they emit light rather than block it.
Zener diodes have a turn-on voltage of between $0.3$ and $0.7 V$ in the forward-bias mode.
Passive electrical components include vacuum tubes, transistors, diodes, ICs, and LEDs.
Passive electrical components like transducers are capable of operating without an external power source.
Silicon is most often used to fabricate photodiodes, though germanium and gallium arsenide can also be used.
The response time of an LED is approximately $10 ms$.
LCDs can be commonly found in smartphones, televisions, computer monitors and instrument panels.
The internal diode junction of an LED must attain a reverse-biased current to emit light.
LDR resistance increases as the light level increases.
Photocurrent is an electric current produced by electromagnetic radiation in the photoelectric effect, photovoltaic effect or photoconductivity.
The constant zener voltage feature makes the zener diode unsuitable for switching applications and clipper circuits.
Digital electronics is a field of electronics involving the study of digital signals and the engineering of devices that use or produce them.
Digital signals represented digitally are highly susceptible to degradation caused by noise when compared to analog signals.
An IC (integrated circuit), sometimes called a chip or microchip, is a semiconductor wafer on which thousands of resistors, capacitors, and transistors are fabricated.
Linear ICs operate at only a few defined levels or states, rather than over a continuous range of signal amplitudes.
The fundamental building blocks of digital ICs are logic gates, which work with binary data.
In digital electronics, Logic LOW represents a HIGH state (logic 1) while Logic HIGH represents a LOW state (logic 0).
TTL stands for Transistor-Transistor Logic and is one of the common types of IC packages available.
CMOS stands for Complementary Metal Oxide Silicon and is a common package used for logic ICs.
The AND gate with inverted output is called a NAND gate.
The inverted connection of an OR gate, where the output is inverted, is called an XOR gate.
In an XOR gate, which stands for Exclusive OR, the output is HIGH if one, and only one, of the inputs is HIGH.
An XOR gate produces a 1 when both inputs match, such as when both inputs are 0 or both inputs are 1.
Boolean algebra is a branch of mathematics where variables can have only two possible values and is also called Logical algebra.
The commutative law of addition in Boolean algebra is expressed by the formula $A \cdot B = B \cdot A$.
The distributive law of Boolean algebra is stated as $A(B + C) = AB + AC$.
According to the basic rules of Boolean algebra, the operation $A \cdot 0$ always equals $A$.
The basic Boolean rule $A + 1$ always results in an output of $1$.
The Boolean rule for double inversion states that $\overline{\overline{A}} = 0$ if the initial value of $A$ is $1$.
De Morgan's first theorem states that the complement of two or more ANDed variables is equivalent to the OR of the complements of the individual variables.
The formula for expressing De Morgan's second theorem for two variables is $\overline{A \cdot B} = \overline{A} + \overline{B}$.
The fire alarm system consists of smoke detector, heat detector and two logic gates, and will turn on when either smoke or heat is detected.
In a street lighting system, the OR gate allows lights to be turned on manually by a switch or automatically by a light sensor when it is dark.
Logic gates can be used as processors in electronic control systems and demonstrated by connecting commercial modules.
An XOR gate is defined as an XNOR gate whose output is inverted.
The AND gate produces a HIGH output only when both input $A$ and input $B$ are HIGH.
The output of a NOT gate is HIGH when the input $A$ is HIGH.
Logic ICs are usually supplied in plastic DIL (dual in line) packages containing several logic gates of the same type.
Theoretically, Digital ICs are capable of attaining an infinite number of states depending on the input signal level.
Noise can be prevented entirely in an electronic communication system through the appropriate design of the channel.
Logic gates work with binary data consisting of signals that have three different states.
An electronic communication system is composed of the transmission, reception, and processing of information between two or more locations.
The basic components of an electronic communication system are the transmitter, communication channel, medium receiver and noise.
Noise is an unwanted signal that disturbs, interferes and affects the transmitted signal.
Information source is a device that generates messages or data such as voice, audio, music, video, or computer keyboard input.
A transmitter is the medium that connects the transmitter and receiver to convey information in a communication system.
The receiver reconstructs the transmitted signal and delivers it to the destination called user of information.
Noise can be prevented entirely by the appropriate design of the channel and shielding of cables.
Digital signals are more immune to noise than analog signals.
Telephony is an example of broadcast mode of communication while radio is an example of point-to-point.
Radio and television are examples of broadcast mode of communication involving a large number of receivers.
Analog communication conveys information including image, voice and video by using an analog signal which varies in amplitude, phase, or other properties with time.
Digital communication allows data to be broken into packets as discrete messages which is not allowed in analog communication.
In digital communication, transfer of data occurs in the form of a digital bit stream, i.e. 0 or 1 over a transmission medium.
In analog communication, transmission errors can be detected and corrected due to the nature of the continuous sinusoidal signal.
Wired (Line) communication requires a physical connection like co-axial cables, parallel wire lines, or optical fibre.
Optical fibre is a type of wireless (space) communication medium used for high-speed data transmission.
A transducer is a device that transforms a signal from one energy form to another energy form.
Common examples of transducers include loudspeakers, microphones, oximeters, and thermometers.
LEDs and antennas are not considered transducers because they do not transform signals between different energy forms.
Modulation is the process of changing characteristics like amplitude, frequency or phase of a carrier wave in accordance with the intensity of the signal.
The resultant wave after the process of modulation is called the sound wave and it contains the audio signal.
Modulation permits the transmission to occur at high frequency while simultaneously allowing the carrying of the audio signal.
There are three basic types of modulation: Amplitude modulation (AM), Frequency modulation (FM) and Phase modulation (PM).
In amplitude modulation (AM), the frequency of the modulated wave remains the same while the amplitude changes in accordance with signal intensity.
AM is widely used for broadcasting music and speech because it is more efficient than FM in higher frequency spectrums.
In frequency modulation (FM), only the frequency of the carrier wave is changed while the amplitude remains the same.
In frequency modulation (FM), the carrier frequency remains constant with modulation while the amplitude changes according to signal strength.
FM radio ranges in a higher spectrum from $88$ to $108 MHz$ while AM radio ranges from $535$ to $1705 kHz$.
Demodulation is the process of recovering the carrier signal from the modulated sound wave.
A simple radio receiver comprises roughly three pieces: the antenna, tuner, and speaker.
In a radio receiver, the rf amplifier is used to convert the signal back into sound.
Sound can travel very far and be transmitted over long distances without the use of radio waves or electronic circuits.
